avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Good stability in all locations. The snowpack will remain moist and well bonded. Any remaining cornices expected to weaken over the forecast period. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: No precipitation is forecast. Remaining cool or mild depending on elevation.
Observed Weather Influences: Mild at all elevations overnight although cooler surface conditions for a time on heavily shaded locations. Bright and mild during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: Good stability in all locations. Firm snow surface conditions in heavily shaded aspects for a time but softer snow everywhere by midday. Residual cornices, especially over East aspects, remain weak. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable throughout. The greatest extent of snow will be above 950 metres around coire back walls, ridge flanks and the plateau. Snow patches and ribbons of well bonded snow will remain in depressions and hollows below. Any remaining cornices could collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Dry cloudy conditions are expected throughout the forecast period.  Strong South-Westerly winds will decrease and become light to moderate through the day.  The freezing level will remain above the summits, with the coolest period being just around dawn.
Observed Weather Influences: A sunny day with strong Westerly winds. The freezing level remained well above the highest summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is well bonded and stable throughout. Shaded aspects are generally firmer than slopes exposed to direct sunlight where it\'s softening at all altitudes. The greatest extent of snow is above 950 metres around coire back walls, ridge flanks and the plateau.  Snow patches and ribbons of well bonded snow remain in depressions and hollows below. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to slowly consolidate and stability will be good in all locations and elevations. The surface will become firm overnight, softening during the day. The greatest continuous snow amounts will exist mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres patchy cover below this level. Any remaining cornices will be unstable affecting slopes below. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: High pressure continues to bring dry conditions through the period with partly clear overnight skies which may produce some patchy frost in sheltered glens. The main freezing level will remain well above all the summits. The moderate South-Westerly winds will ease slightly but remain South-Westerly.
Observed Weather Influences: Dry throughout the forecast period. Sunny during the day with the freezing level well above the summits and light variable winds which were mostly Southerly.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack surface is slowly thawing and softening but the overall snowpack stability remains good. The greatest continuous snow amounts exist mainly on North to Easterly aspects above 850 metres patchy cover below this level. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will continue to slowly thaw, be well consolidated and stable. Most extensive areas will be found on North through East to South-East aspects above 800 metres in the higher coires. Other areas on the mountains are largely snow free. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: There will be a small amount of rain overnight, then a mainly dry and cloudy day during Saturday with a slight chance of some drizzle. South Westerly winds, will decrease to moderate by dawn, then increase to Fresh in the afternoon. Freezing level will remain above the summits throughout.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry day with a mix of cloud and sunshine. Winds were South South-Westerly and Very Strong to Gale force on the tops. Freezing level was well above the summits, with air temperatures around 5 to 6 degrees Celsius at munro level.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and is well consolidated. Most extensive snow amounts can be found above 800 metres, on North through East to South-East aspects in the higher coires. The snow here is generally softening in the warmer temperatures, with some high shaded areas maintaining a firmer surface. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will remain well bonded and stable. Large cornices remain above some North to East aspects and these will be soft particularly if in the sun with collapse possible.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A mild, dry, overcast day with moderate South-South-Westerly winds. The freezing level will be above the summits.
Observed Weather Influences: A mild dry day with moderate South-South-Westerly winds. The freezing level was well above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack remains well bonded and stable. The snow surface softened through the day particularly on sun affected slopes. Some large cornices remain above some North to East aspects, these cornices are soft where in the sun and collapse is possible.  The avalanche hazard is Low

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be well bonded and stable in all locations. Firming up slightly overnight the snowpack will soften during the day, especially where exposed to direct sunlight. Shaded aspects remaining firmer. Greatest snow cover will be found on high plateaus and on mainly North to East aspects above 850 metres and in deep depressions at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Remaining dry with variable moderate winds. The freezing level will remain above the highest summits.
Observed Weather Influences: A dry, sunny day with light winds. The freezing level was above the highest summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack is stable and well bonded throughout. Initially firm turning soft during the morning with the exception of shady areas. Greatest amounts are found above 850 metres on mainly North to East aspects and on high plateaus. Snow cover is becoming more broken in the mild conditions. The avalanche hazard is Low.
